user_message: Just came out of competitive positioning with Alpine Ski House. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-02-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Alpine Ski House)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] David (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:17:00] David (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:21:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:26:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:34:00] David (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:37:00] David (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades
